text.title: mobile-ux Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for mobile-ux as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Shrinking desktop design to fit mobile viewport instead of redesigning for mobile context.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Produces tiny touch targets, cramped layouts, and forced-zoom content. Mobile is a different context requiring different information hierarchy, not a smaller copy of the desktop layout.

failure-mode.description: Skeleton screens replaced with generic spinners for content-heavy pages.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Skeleton screens reduce perceived load time for known-layout content because they set accurate expectations. Generic spinners with unknown duration increase perceived wait times and anxiety.
